news.summary: The trading revenues of a major cryptocurrency exchange decreased by 19% compared to the previous quarter due to difficulties affecting the cryptocurrency markets.
news.headline: Coinbase's Q1 revenue misses forecast as trading volume drops, reflecting a 19% decrease from the previous quarter amidst market challenges.

news.summary: First-quarter results were reported with a net investment income of $0.35 per share and a net asset value of $29.66 per share.
news.headline: Logan Ridge Finance Corp. posts strong Q1 2025 results: $0.35 net investment income/share and $29.66 net asset value/share.

news.summary: Interfor Corporation reported a Net loss of $35.1 million in Q1'25, with EBITDA at $48.6 million on $735.5 million in sales. Lumber prices improved, with an average selling price of $712 per mfbm, and lumber shipments decreased to 863 million board feet in Q1'25. The company's net debt was $886.3 million, with available liquidity at $306.0 million. They monetized Coastal B.C. operations and sold Quebec operations. Capital spending was $17.2 million, with $7.5 million focused on the Thomaston, GA sawmill rebuild. They recorded $22.3 million in duties expenses in the quarter. Interfor maintained compliance with financial covenants and believed it had sufficient working capital and liquidity for future funding.
news.headline: Interfor reported Q1'25 with $49M Adjusted EBITDA and $35M net loss. Notable items: Lumber prices up, reduced shipments. Financial position stable with $886.3M net debt, $306M liquidity. Sold Coastal B.C. operations for $7.4M gain. Forecasting volatile lumber markets in the near term.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, Definity Financial (TSX: DFY) reported a 9.6% growth in gross written premium, reaching $1.03 billion. The combined ratio stood at 94.5%, with operating net income at $75.9 million and an operating EPS of $0.65. Net income attributable to common shareholders was $92 million. Book value per share increased by 16.2% to $29.52. Gross written premiums grew by $74.5 million (7.8%) compared to Q1 2024, primarily driven by personal auto and property lines. Underwriting income was $55 million, impacted by winter weather conditions. Net investment income rose by $1.6 million, and distribution income increased to $11 million. Personal insurance gross written premiums were up 6.7%, while commercial insurance saw a 10% increase in premiums. Despite more active winter weather in 2025, operating ROE showed improvement at 10.3%. Definity remains optimistic about reaching its financial targets in 2025 and becoming a top five P&C insurer. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Definity Financial Corp. reports Q1 2025 results: $1,030.1M gross premiums, 9.6% growth; $75.9M operating income, 10.3% ROE.

news.summary: CES Energy Solutions Corp. reported strong financial results for Q1 2025, with record quarterly revenue of $632.4 million, a 7% increase year over year. Adjusted EBITDAC was $99.9 million, and the company returned $28.1 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. CES' performance was driven by its unique business model, providing essential chemical solutions for drilling operations. The company saw increases in revenue in both the US ($402.5 million) and Canada ($230.0 million) markets. Despite a slight decrease in net income to $44.1 million, CES generated $77.8 million in Funds Flow from Operations. The company had a Working Capital Surplus of $686.8 million and Total Debt of $469.2 million as of March 31, 2025. CES remains optimistic about future growth prospects, with a focus on maintaining strong financial performance and capital structure.
news.headline: CES Energy Solutions Corp reported a record revenue of $632.4 million in Q1 2025, a 7% increase year over year. $99.9 million Adjusted EBITDAC was achieved with 1.17x Total Debt/Adjusted EBITDAC. Quarterly dividend declared at $0.0425 per share. Business model supported strong surplus free cash flow. Detailed financial highlights available on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: ContextLogic (NASDAQ: LOGC) is set to reveal its quarterly earnings on Friday, May 9, 2025. Analysts forecast an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.04 for ContextLogic. The upcoming announcement has generated excitement among investors who are eager to see if the company exceeds expectations. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: ContextLogic (NASDAQ: LOGC) to release quarterly earnings on 2025-05-09, analysts expect EPS of $0.04. Investors eager for results.

news.summary: Sangoma Technologies Corporation, a leading provider of Communications as a Service solutions, based in Markham, Ontario, released its financial results for the third quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The company, traded on TSX as STC and Nasdaq as SANG, presented unaudited condensed interim financial statements for the three and nine month periods. Sangoma offers businesses choices between on-premises, cloud-based, or hybrid solutions. The quarter's results demonstrate the company's dedication and strategic approach to operations.
news.headline: Sangoma reported Q3 fiscal 2025 results, offering various communication solutions. Financials for the three and nine months ended March 31, 2025, are out.

news.summary: IGM Financial Inc. reported record-high first quarter earnings in 2025, with adjusted net earnings of $237.8 million, a 5.9% increase from the previous year. Adjusted earnings per share were $1.00, up from $0.94 in 2024. Net earnings were $233.8 million, a 4.7% increase year-over-year. Assets under management and advisement reached $503.6 billion, up 19.1% from the end of 2024. IG Wealth Management saw record gross client inflows of $4.2 billion, while Mackenzie Investments onboarded $3.6 billion of institutional assets. The company's dividend per share was declared at 56.25 cents. Certain financial measures and ratios are non-IFRS and may not be directly comparable to other companies. Businesses like Wealth Management and Asset Management showed growth in assets under management and client inflows, contributing to the positive performance. Investment fund net redemptions decreased, and ETF assets under management also rose. The Company emphasizes forward-looking statements, detailing risks and uncertainties, and provides additional information on non-IFRS financial measures in its disclosure materials.
news.headline: IGM Financial Inc. reports Q1 2025 earnings: $237.8M adjusted net earnings, $275.0B AUM, $4.2B net inflows, $124.3M for Wealth Management, and $86.5M for Asset Management. Quarter-end total assets under management of $503.6B, with $141.5B for IG Wealth Management, and $218.6B for Mackenzie Investments. Quarter-end ETF assets under management at $17.0B. Net earnings of $233.8M and earnings per share of $0.98. Dividend of 56.25 cents per share declared. Non-IFRS measures used. Website for more info: www.sedarplus.ca.

news.summary: Wolfspeed, Inc. (NYSE: WOLF) recently unveiled its third-quarter results following Thursday's market close. Highlights from the report include quarterly losses amounting to 72 cents per share, surpassing analysts' predictions of 77 cents in losses. Despite this, the company's quarterly revenue stood at $185.4 million, falling short of the anticipated $194.13 million and showing a decline from the $200.7 million revenue recorded in the corresponding period last year. For further insights, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Wolfspeed Inc. (NYSE: WOLF) Q3: Loss of 72 cents/share (est. 77 cents), revenue $185.4M (est. $194.13M), down from $200.7M YoY.

news.summary: DraftKings Inc (NASDAQ: DKNG) released its first-quarter financial results, showing a revenue of $1.41 billion, a 20% increase from the previous year. This figure fell short of the estimated $1.44 billion. Earnings per share were reported at 12 cents, missing estimated 22 cents per share. The growth in revenue was driven by increased customer engagement, new customer acquisition, a higher sportsbook hold percentage, and the impact of acquiring Jackpocket. Monthly Unique Payers (MUPs) rose by 28% to 4.3 million in Q1, attributed to strong retention, acquisition, and the Jackpocket acquisition. Excluding Jackpocket, MUPs would have...
news.headline: DraftKings Q1 earnings: Revenue of $1.41B (20% YoY), EPS misses, MUPs up 28% to 4.3M. Guidance cut due to bettors beating house.

news.headline: Guardian Capital Group Limited (TSX: GCG; GCG.A) reports its 2025 Q1 operating results in Toronto.

news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, Pembina Pipeline Corporation (TSX: PPL; NYSE: PBA) released its financial and operational performance for Q1 2025. The company reported earnings of $502 million for the quarter, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $1,167 million and adjusted cash flow from operating activities at $777 million ($1.34 per share). Additionally, the board of directors approved an increase in the common share cash dividend.
news.headline: Pembina Pipeline Corp reports Q1 2025 earnings of $502M, EBITDA $1,167M, cash flow $777M, raises dividend.

news.summary: The board of directors of PotlatchDeltic Corporation (Nasdaq: PCH) has announced a quarterly distribution of $0.45 per share on the Company's common stock. This distribution will be payable on June 30, 2025, to stockholders of record as of June 6, 2025. PotlatchDeltic, a prominent Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT), owns 2.1 million acres of timberlands across states including Alabama, Arkansas, Georgia, Idaho, Louisiana, Mississippi, and South Carolina.
news.headline: PotlatchDeltic declared a $0.45 per share distribution on common stock, payable on June 30, 2025, to stockholders of record by June 6. Leading REIT with 2.1M acres of timberlands.

news.summary: In recent news, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has dropped to a record low of 3.7%. This marks a significant decrease from the previous rate of 4.0%. Additionally, the economy added 250,000 jobs last month, exceeding expectations and showcasing strong growth in the labor market. The positive data suggests a thriving economy and robust job market, providing reassurance for both investors and workers. The decrease in unemployment rate and the substantial job growth reflect a promising outlook for the country's economic stability and future growth prospects.
news.headline: Instagram CEO expresses long-standing concern over TikTok competition, citing growing user base and engagement.

news.summary: 4Front Ventures Corp., a cannabis operator, has disclosed that the Ontario Securities Commission (OSC) issued a failure-to-file cease trade order on May 7, 2025. The company, known as 4Front, trades on the CSE under the ticker symbol FFNT and on the OTCQB under the ticker symbol FFNTF. This announcement was made in Phoenix on May 8, 2025.
news.headline: OSC issued a cease trade order against 4Front for not filing, on May 7, 2025.

This dividend is payable on June 30, 2025, to shareholders of Sun Life Financial Inc. The Company's common shares are listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: SLF) and the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SLF).

news.summary: In Chicago, Cresco Labs has disclosed the rescheduling of their first quarter 2025 earnings release and conference call. The company has decided to postpone the announcement, and shareholders and investors can now anticipate updated financial results and a conference call at a later date. The rescheduling affects the presentation of crucial figures for the first quarter of 2025, impacting stakeholders who rely on this data for investment decisions and market assessments.
news.headline: Cresco Labs in Chicago postpones First Quarter 2025 earnings release & call.

news.headline: Wheaton Precious Metals announces quarterly dividend increase to US$0.165 per share, up 6.5% from last quarter.

Among these events, three will feature fireside chats with company management. Investors interested in accessing these discussions can find them on Broadridge's Investor Relations page.
news.headline: Broadridge (NYSE: BR) to join 5 investor events; 3 fireside chats scheduled with management.

news.summary: In St. Louis on May 8, 2025, Post Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: POST), a consumer packaged goods holding company, announced their second fiscal quarter results as of March 31, 2025. The highlights include second quarter net sales totaling $2.0 billion and an operating profit of $182.2 million.
news.headline: Post Holdings, Inc. reports Q2 results for FY2025: Net sales of $2.0 billion, operating profit $182.2 million.Raises outlook.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, the Board of Directors of Prologis, Inc. (NYSE: PLD) announced a quarterly cash dividend for the period ending June 30, 2025. Shareholders can expect a dividend of $1.01 per share on the company's common stock. This dividend is set to be paid out on June 30, 2025.

news.summary: The Mohegan Tribal Gaming Authority, based in Uncasville, Connecticut, revealed on May 8, 2025, that they have initiated processes to exchange or buy back for cash their existing 13.25% Senior Notes set to mature in 2027. The offer also includes a request for consents on certain matters related to the notes. This move signifies a strategic financial decision, potentially involving a significant amount of debt restructuring or refinancing.
news.headline: Mohegan starts offers to exchange or buy back its 13.25% Senior Notes due 2027, also seeking consents.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, Bit Digital, Inc. (Nasdaq: BTBT), a leading global provider of high-performance computing infrastructure and digital asset production based in New York, revealed its plans to unveil the First Quarter financial results. The company specializes in HPC services and is recognized for its expertise in the digital asset space. Headquartered in New York, Bit Digital operates on a global scale and holds a prominent position in the industry. Investors and stakeholders can anticipate the forthcoming release of the financial performance data for the First Quarter.
news.headline: Bit Digital, Inc. (Nasdaq: BTBT) sets First Quarter financial results release for 2025, with a conference call.

news.summary: Experienced Energy Professional with Extensive Industry and Strategic Knowledge Joins Prologis, Inc. On May 8, 2025, Sarah Slusser was appointed to the board of directors of Prologis, Inc. (NYSE: PLD). The decision was made during the company's yearly stockholder meeting. Slusser's election brings valuable expertise, enhancing the board's capabilities.

news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, on May 8, 2025, TC Energy Corporation shared in a press release that during their 2025 annual shareholder meeting, 13 nominees were successfully elected as directors of the company. The election was carried out through a vote by ballot. The directors are set to serve until TC Energy's subsequent annual shareholder meeting or until their replacements are chosen. TC Energy, listed on both the Toronto Stock Exchange and the New York Stock Exchange under the symbols TRP, highlighted this significant development.
news.headline: TC Energy announces Board of Directors election results with 13 nominees elected at the 2025 annual meeting.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, MSC Industrial Supply Co., a prominent distributor based in Melville, N.Y., and Davidson, N.C., shared updates about their upcoming investor event. The company, listed on NYSE as MSM, specializes in providing a diverse array of metalworking and maintenance, repair, and operations (MRO) products and services in North America.
news.headline: MSC Industrial Supply Co. (NYSE: MSM) to participate in investor conferences. Key distributor of metalworking & MRO products.

news.summary: Today, Canadian Apartment Properties Real Estate Investment Trust (CAPREIT) disclosed its operational and financial performance for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The company's stock symbol is TSX: CAR.UN. A conference call led by management to delve into these results is scheduled for May 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time.
news.headline: CAPREIT announces Q1 2025 results, to host call on May 9 at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss financial performance.

news.summary: Vancouver, BC - On May 8, 2025, Wheaton announced their impressive first quarter financial results, showcasing a robust kickoff to 2025. The company's core assets outperformed production projections, leading to a notable increase in quarterly revenue, adjusted net earnings, and operating cash flow. This success highlights the company's strong performance despite economic uncertainties.
news.headline: Wheaton Precious Metals reports record revenue, adjusted net earnings, and operating cash flow for Q1 2025. Strong start.

news.summary: Richmond American Homes of Florida, LP, a subsidiary of M.D.C. Holdings, Inc., is excited to unveil Coastal Gardens in Palm Coast on Thursday, May 15. The Grand Opening event is set to take place on this date. More details can be found at RichmondAmerican.com/CoastalGardens.
news.headline: Richmond American Homes' new community, Coastal Gardens, will open on May 15 in Palm Coast.

news.summary: NuVista Energy Ltd., based in Calgary, Alberta and listed on the TSX as NVA, reported robust financial and operational performance for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The company highlighted its steady returns from a high-quality asset portfolio, emphasizing the achievement of new production milestones. Progress was made on the company's NCIB to return capital to shareholders, along with a successful renewal and amendment of their three-year covenant-based credit facility to bolster financial stability. Following a successful Q1, NuVista reaffirmed its annual capital and production guidance, showcasing its strong performance in the industry.
news.headline: NuVista Energy Ltd. reports strong Q1 2025 results, enhances shareholder return strategy with progress on NCIB and credit facility.

news.summary: A recent study revealed that 78% of people surveyed believe that climate change is a significant threat to the world. The survey collected data from 2000 individuals across different countries. Additionally, 65% of participants expressed concern about the lack of action being taken to address environmental issues. The study also found that 82% of respondents felt that governments and corporations should be doing more to combat climate change. These findings highlight a growing global awareness and demand for decisive action to protect the environment.
news.headline: Healthcare company Nova Leap reports Q1 2025 financial performance.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, Kinross Gold Corporation (TSX: K, NYSE: KGC) revealed the outcomes of its Board of Directors election held during the virtual Annual Meeting of Shareholders in Toronto. The specific voting details were disclosed to the public following the event.
news.headline: Kinross Gold Corp. releases election results of Board of Directors after virtual Annual Shareholder Meeting on May 7, 2025.

news.summary: Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Quebec, May 8, 2025 - Colabor Group Inc. (TSX: GCL) conducted its annual shareholder meeting on this day. A notable turnout was seen, with 61,043,516 common shares, equivalent to 59.84% of the total shares, being represented either in person or through proxy at the gathering.
news.headline: Colabor Group reports results of shareholder votes: 61.04 million shares (representing 59.84%) were represented at the meeting.

news.summary: In the past year, the Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) has surged by 54% year-over-year to reach $73.7 million. The Total Revenue has also seen significant growth, increasing by 42% year-over-year to reach $21.7 million. Additionally, the Adjusted EBITDA has climbed by 39% year-over-year to reach $5.6 million.
news.headline: VitalHub's Q1 2025 results: ARR up 54% YoY to $73.7M, Total Revenue up 42% YoY to $21.7M, Adjusted EBITDA up 39% YoY to $5.6M.
ECN Capital's Q1-2025 Adjusted Net Income per Common Share stands at 3 cents.

news.summary: Leading biomedical innovator Femasys Inc. (NASDAQ: FEMY) is making strides in transforming women’s health globally with their innovative solutions. This includes FemaSeed® for first-line infertility treatment and FemBloc® for non-surgical permanent birth control, awaiting full system approval in Europe. The company, based in Atlanta, announced their financial results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. Femasys Inc. is focused on addressing significant unmet needs in women’s health, offering a range of disruptive, in-office therapeutic and diagnostic products.
news.headline: Femasys reports Q1 2025 financial results, highlighting FemaSeed for infertility and FemBloc for birth control. (characters: 97)

news.summary: The net operating income (NOI) and rent per square foot (PSF) both experienced an increase across the entire portfolio. The same property NOI saw a significant rise, along with the rent PSF, showcasing positive growth in financial performance. This indicates a healthy trend with a notable improvement in the revenue generated by the properties. These increases reflect successful management strategies and market conditions that have positively impacted the financial metrics of the portfolio.
news.headline: Firm Capital Property Trust's Q1/2025 results show increases in Same Property NOI and Rent PSF across the entire portfolio.

news.summary: The Starlight Western Canada Multi-Family (No. 2) Fund reported its Q1-2025 financial results with $5,460 revenue, $3,797 net operating income, and 96.1% physical occupancy across 9 properties. There was a 3.9% increase in average monthly rent (AMR) and $245 net income for Q1-2025. The Fund had $6,353 liquidity and collected 99.2% of rents for the quarter. Adjusted funds from operations (AFFO) for Q1-2025 were $979, a 140.0% increase from Q1-2024. Key financial stats as at March 31, 2025, included $414,646 gross book value, $268,531 indebtedness, and 64.8% indebtedness to gross book value ratio. Weighted average interest rate was 3.25% and average loan term to maturity was 4.84 years.
news.headline: Starlight Western Canada Multi-Family (No. 2) Fund reports 4.2% NOI growth in Q1-2025, AMR up 3.9%, 96.1% occupancy. AFFO at $979, liquidity $6,353.
